stories.headline: Trump says US will hit Iran 'hard' again today
stories.summary: The US president warns Iran "will have to pay the price" for taking too long to agree a deal, after Tehran vows retaliation to any attacks.

stories.headline: Trump warns US will soon hit Iran ‘hard’ again
stories.summary: President’s remarks come after countries exchange fire following downing of American helicopter

stories.headline: Trump warns of more attacks on Iran after US Apache helicopter downing
stories.summary: Trump warns of more attacks on Iran after US Apache helicopter downing

stories.headline: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons in visit to Guantánamo Bay
stories.summary: US defense secretary continues ramp-up of pressure against country including sanctions and devastating oil blockadePete Hegseth has warned Cuba against acquiring weapons that could threaten the United States, during a visit to the US military base at Guantánamo Bay.Washington has ramped up pressure against Cuba with sanctions and a devastating oil blockade, and Donald Trump has repeatedly signaled that the Cuban government could be the next after

stories.headline: Zelensky under pressure to end row with Poland over WW2 name of army unit
stories.summary: Poland is threatening to strip Ukraine's president of a state honour over the naming Ukrainian military unit after controversial World War Two fighters.

stories.headline: Trump vows fresh attacks on Iran and accuses negotiators of 'playing us for suckers'
stories.summary: The US launched air strikes against Iran early on Wednesday and Tehran fired back at countries in the region in escalating attacks that threatened to derail efforts to end the war.

stories.headline: Kyiv hit Russian military plant using Ukrainian-made Flamingo missile, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy vowed to continue Kyiv’s strikes deep inside Russia in response to attacks on Ukraine, but also a way of forcing the Kremlin into direct talks.

stories.headline: US military chief Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring military arms
stories.summary: Hegseth's visit to Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, comes as the Trump administration increases pressure against Cuba's government.

stories.headline: 'Children are dying,' Cuba says as US blockade hampers delivery of UN aid
stories.summary: The blockade, coupled with expanded US sanctions that punish companies doing business with the Cuban state, has intensified the worst economic and energy crisis on the island in over a generation.

stories.headline: Bill Gates says Epstein wanted personal relationship, but he 'never reciprocated'
stories.summary: The Microsoft co-founder spent hours talking to lawmakers behind closed doors, where he also said Epstein used his marital infidelities to pressure him.

stories.headline: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
stories.summary: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North SumatraExtreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival.The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans (Pongo tapanuliensis) were kil

stories.headline: West Bank ethnic cleansing, settler attacks Israel’s state policy: Amnesty
stories.summary: Israel uses war crimes to accelerate 'annexation agenda and settlement expansion' in occupied land, rights group says.

stories.headline: Deadly clashes in Kenya as protesters fight US Ebola quarantine facility
stories.summary: Kenya saw fresh unrest on 9 June as protesters defied a court order and clashed with police near Laikipia Air Base over plans for a US funded Ebola quarantine facility, fuelling fears about the virus and prompting arrests.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

stories.headline: Ukraine says missiles hit military plant deep inside Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine's military also says it has struck a Russian oil refinery and a "shadow fleet" oil tanker in the Black Sea.

stories.headline: Trump Officials Say ICE Won’t Raid World Cup Games, but Fans Are Worried
stories.summary: Federal agents are there for security, not immigration enforcement, officials say. But trust is fragile after the administration’s recent crackdowns.

stories.headline: Israeli strike on a car kills two in Sidon, Lebanon
stories.summary: An Israeli strike on a vehicle in the southern Lebanese city of Sidon killed two people and sparked a fire.

stories.headline: Bill Gates testifies in closed-door US House meeting over Epstein ties
stories.summary: In prepared remarks, Gates told the House Oversight Committee that meeting Epstein was a 'grave error in judgment'.

stories.headline: Bill Gates appears before Congress to testify over Epstein files
stories.summary: Microsoft founder Bill Gates appeared before Congress to testify over Epstein files

stories.headline: Sanctions on settlers not enough: Target Israeli gov’t, say campaigners
stories.summary: Campaigners argue that sanctions reflect a need to manage public anger rather than a genuine shift in state policy.

stories.headline: Analysis: Kim Jong Un emerges as winner in summit with Xi Jinping
Anthropic plugs Claude AI in Japan for automated software development
Malaysia's Anwar warns against global powers weaponizing trade
'Partners and friends’: Trade and defence top of agenda at EU-South Korea summit
stories.summary: As security threats grow and geopolitics shift, the European Union and Republic of South Korea have celebrated a new Digital Trade Agreement at a summit in Brussels.

stories.headline: Hong Kong charges seven people and two firms over massive fire that killed 168
stories.summary: The massive blaze, which engulfed seven of the eight high-rise apartment blocks at the Wang Fuk Court estate in November, prompted a months-long investigation into the cause.

stories.headline: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman
stories.summary: India's government said three Indian sailors were missing and 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

stories.headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
stories.summary: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

stories.headline: Growing backlash in Japan over Trump's use of anime characters
stories.summary: The US president has angered some fans by using the images of characters like Pikachu and Naruto.

stories.headline: Alleged Bondi Beach gunman charged with another 19 offences
stories.summary: Naveed Akram, 24, is already facing 59 charges over an attack on a Jewish festival in December.

stories.headline: Oil tankers increase ‘dark’ transits through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Donald Trump says US launched ‘secret mission’ to help move crude through the waterway

stories.headline: What happened to the great rebirth of American manufacturing?
stories.summary: Skills shortages, shifting tariffs and complex permitting processes are major barriers

stories.headline: Germany’s €100bn bid to make the trains run on time
stories.summary: The country’s railway renewal is the first test of whether Europe’s largest economy can reverse years of decline

stories.headline: Why Nike Keeps Stumbling
stories.summary:
The return of Elliott Hill to lead Nike was supposed to reinvigorate the brand. Instead, setbacks in running, product development and brand strategy have kept the swoosh from once again hitting its stride.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: SpaceX Tells Investors It Has Lined Up Blue-Chip Credit Ratings
stories.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX is telling investors it has lined up investment-grade ratings from three major bond graders, according to people with knowledge of the matter, which could help the company cut funding costs as it continues to raise financing after its initial public offering.

stories.headline: Chevron Among Drillers to Feed Key Argentina Shale NGL Venture
stories.summary: Chevron Corp. and two other major Argentine shale producers will sign contracts this week to supply a natural gas liquids project, a move that all but assures the $3 billion plan goes ahead, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Major US Banks Face Federal Probe Over Debanking Allegations
stories.summary: Federal prosecutors are looking into whether some of Wall Street’s biggest lenders illegally dropped customers for political reasons, according to a person familiar with the probe.

stories.headline: The Golden Age of IPOs is Here: IPOX's Schuster
stories.summary:
Josef Schuster, founder and CEO of IPOX Schuster, joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Deals." SpaceX is offering 555.6 million shares at a fixed price of $135 each, which would raise about $75 billion. The stock is set to start trading on June 12. The IPO is expected to rank as the biggest ever, topping Saudi Aramco’s $29.4 billion listing in 2019.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: SpaceX Price Tag is 'Very Steep': Renaissance's Kennedy
stories.summary:
Matt Kennedy, senior strategist at Renaissance Capital joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Deals." SpaceX is offering 555.6 million shares at a fixed price of $135 each, which would raise about $75 billion. The stock is set to start trading on June 12. The IPO is expected to rank as the biggest ever, topping Saudi Aramco’s $29.4 billion listing in 2019.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UK’s Reeves to Lower Tax Burden for Wealthy US Expats
stories.summary: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said she will ease the tax burden faced by rich US investors wanting to relocate to the UK.

stories.headline: CIBC to Offer SpaceX Access Through Depositary Receipt
stories.summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce is set to offer Canadians access to SpaceX through a vehicle that gives local investors access to global stocks.

stories.headline: Ipsos Poll Shows Majority of Adults Would Rejoin EU
stories.summary: Alastair Campbell, co-host The Rest Is Politics, and Sir Jacob Rees-Mogg, presenter for GB News, talk about what could happen if there was a vote to rejoin the EU. They speak to Mishal Husain at a Bloomberg.com subscriber-only event held in London on June 10. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Metals Drop on Renewed Middle East Tensions, US Rate Outlook
stories.summary: Metals from aluminum to zinc dropped after tensions in the Middle East escalated and expectations for US rate hikes weighed on the demand outlook for commodities.

stories.headline: SpaceX Leaves Some Banks Peeved at Junior Roles in IPO Lineup
stories.summary: Several junior banks marketing shares in SpaceX’s landmark listing are working for relatively small fees and being relegated to roles that don’t come with any credit, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: The vanishing of Nicolás Maduro: how the former dictator is being erased from Venezuela
stories.summary: Billboards are being painted over and former allies seem eager to forget the man they once glorifiedFor years, his bewhiskered face stared down from propaganda billboards glorifying the supposedly revolutionary rule of a dictator who styled himself as “the protector of the people”.The spin-doctored adoration was such that factories churned out plastic action figures exalting Nicolás Maduro as an “indestructible” and “iron-fisted” caped crusader n

stories.headline: Labor scraps plan to make spy agency’s 9/11-era questioning powers permanent

Pakistan army helicopter crashes in Kashmir after technical fault, killing all on board
stories.summary: Witnesses said that the helicopter crashed shortly after take-off from a helipad. Ambulances arrived at the scene and transported the victims to a nearby hospital.

stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
stories.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

stories.headline: Oil Jumps as Trump Threatens to ‘Hit Iran Hard’ Imminently
stories.summary: Oil turned higher as President Donald Trump warned of imminent US attacks against Iran and domestic crude inventories posted another steep drop.

stories.headline: Man reportedly shot at Kenya protest against US Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: Protesters are concerned about cross-border infection risks and the lack of transparency from the government about the treatment centre.

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.summary: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plansKenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens.Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.

stories.headline: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
stories.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

stories.headline: Israeli air strikes hit Lebanese city of Tyre despite Iranian warning to stop attacks
stories.summary: Iran warned Israel on Monday that it could resume hostilities if attacks on its Lebanese ally Hezbollah do not stop.

stories.headline: Two reportedly killed as women take part in rare protest in Afghanistan
stories.summary: The demonstration, in the western city of Herat, was broken up after police are alleged to have opened fire.

stories.headline: Murder of Lyhanna, 11, enrages France and turns up heat on government
stories.summary: Protesters are angry that the suspect had already been reported to police last August in a separate case.

stories.headline: Version of AI tool 'too powerful for public' released to public
stories.summary: Claude Fable 5 is a version of Anthropic's Claude Mythos, an AI program which caused a stir among technology, finance, and government leaders.

stories.headline: Tech Stocks Sink as Oil Jumps on US-Iran Jitters: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A flare-up in geopolitical tensions lifted oil prices while fueling losses in stocks and bonds, with Wall Street also rattled by a renewed selloff in some the world’s largest technology companies.

stories.headline: Argentina’s Impsa Is in Talks to Resume Turbine Work in Venezuela
stories.summary: Argentina’s Impsa SA is in talks with the Venezuelan government to restart manufacturing and repairs on turbines for the Andean nation’s sprawling Guri dam complex, according to the company’s top executive.

stories.headline: Russia's fuel crisis intensifies as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
stories.summary: Kyiv has made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to the territories it occupies.

stories.headline: Zelensky thanks UK for 'ironclad' support after meeting King
stories.summary: Following their meeting, Zelensky thanks the UK for its "ironclad" support for  Ukraine.

stories.headline: Couple arrested in Spain over gangland murder bid
stories.summary: The couple are alleged to have shot the victim four times on his doorstep in the San Javier district of Santiago de la Ribera in November 2024.

stories.headline: Watch: Why is China's Xi Jinping visiting North Korea?
stories.summary: The Chinese President Xi received a colourful welcome to Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang on trip to revitalise China-North Korea ties
stories.summary: Kim Jong-un welcomes Chinese leader on visit to renew relations strained amid Pyongyang’s closeness with RussiaXi Jinping has arrived in North Korea for a two-day trip, his first in nearly seven years, as China’s leader looks to revitalise ties with his junior ally.Footage published by China’s Xinhua state news agency showed an Air China plane carrying Xi and his wife, Peng Liyuan, touching down at Pyongyang’s Sunan international airport. stories.headline: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
stories.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.

stories.headline: How one of India's most successful female politicians is losing her party
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee's once-dominant Trinamool Congress party is unravelling, weeks after losing power in West Bengal.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Monsoon season brings vital rainfall to parts of Asia
stories.summary: India declares onset as up to 280mm of rain falls in 72 hours in Kerala, while downpours hit south-west ThailandThe monsoon season has officially begun in parts of Asia, marking the start of a period of enhanced rainfall vital to the region’s economy.The south-west monsoon begins each year as a consequence of a growing temperature difference between the Asian land mass and the Indian Ocean. Through spring, the land heats up more rapidly than the

stories.headline: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
stories.summary: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

stories.headline: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
stories.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
stories.summary: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on
stories.summary: Fifty years after his debut, Ilaiyaraaja remains one of the most celebrated composers in Indian cinema.

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Delcy Rodríguez visits India: Will oil talks lead to an energy deal?
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez's India visit highlights Venezuela's growing role in Delhi's oil diversification.
